full_text_rationale: Full text describes K3 Trans constructing and continuously evolving a translation knowledge base from target-language codebases, repository context, and prior translation results, with repository-level code-translation evaluation.
titulo: Evolving Triple Knowledge-Augmented LLMs for Code Translation in Repository Context
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 005; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Guangsheng Ou, Mingwei Liu, Yuxuan Chen, Xueying Du, Shengbo Wang, Zekai Zhang, Xin Peng, Zibin Zheng
formato_armazenamento: Self-evolving triple translation knowledge base containing dependency usage examples from the repository, target-language code samples from existing projects, and successful translation function pairs from prior translation history.
benchmarks_utilizados: RustRepoTrans repository-level C-to-Rust code translation benchmark
metricas_desempenho: Compilation@1, Pass@1, DSR@1, Repairable Ratio, CodeBLEU, AST Match Score
baseline_comparado: Basic RAG adapted from prior work, prompt engineering baseline, C2Rust rule-based baseline, ablations over knowledge types and retrieval strategy.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Knowledge base is evaluated at 0%, 50%, and 100% self-evolution stages; 100% corresponds to full K3 Trans.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; implementation limits LLM repair to one iteration for cost-effectiveness but reports no monetary cost.
resultado_principal: K3 Trans improves repository-context code translation with triple knowledge retrieval and self-debugging. With Claude-3-5-Sonnet, K3 Trans reaches 75.7% Compilation@1, 67.7% Pass@1, 82.1% DSR@1, 44.6% RR, 0.733 CodeBLEU, and 0.562 AST match.
desempenho_vs_baseline: For Claude-3-5-Sonnet, K3 Trans improves over prompt engineering from 57.9% to 75.7% Compilation@1 and from 49.1% to 67.7% Pass@1; the paper reports relative improvements up to 135.9% on Pass@1 and 32.8% on CodeBLEU among studied LLMs.
limitacoes: Too many target-language code samples can introduce noise; benchmark target language is Rust only; possible data leakage between RustRepoTrans and target-language sample sources; one-iteration repair is used for cost-effectiveness.
ameacas_validade: Rust-only benchmark, possible overlap between benchmark and retrieved code samples, retrieval strategy settings, translation order randomness, and LLM choice may affect generalizability.
